title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following a death inquest, Alexander, Harold, and Wimburn prepare to depart. Harold expresses impatience due to the presence of cameras and people, while Wimburn expresses displeasure toward Lucy, implying her prying caused the current unfortunate situation. Harold questions Lucy regarding why she opened the coffin; she explains she did so because she smelled an unpleasant odor and felt the area needed cleaning. Wimbourne notes that forensic experts determined the woman had been dead for three weeks.

A disagreement arises between the brothers regarding the deceased's presence in the warehouse. Harold questions why she was there, and Wimbush suggests she may have been on a date, noting that the warehouse key is traditionally hung outside for the Women's Guild's use. Emma explains the key-hanging is a wartime habit for convenience. Cedric and Harold engage in a bickering match regarding Cedric's bohemian attire and the nature of murder in Ibiza versus Britain. Wimbush reprimands the bickering and offers to assist Emma before returning to London. Wimbush proposes a theory that a local couple had a heated argument in the warehouse and the man hid the body in the coffin. Cedric disputes this, noting no locals recognize the woman.

Upon arriving at Rutherford Hall, Wimburn meets Inspector Bacon and Detective Constable Craddock from Scotland Yard. Craddock informs Wimburn that the deceased is not a local but a woman from London who had recently returned from abroad, possibly France. Craddock and Bacon explain that the Commissioner sent Scotland Yard to take over the investigation. Wimburn expresses his hope for a quick resolution to minimize the trouble caused to the Crickenshaw family.
